explanation: This property expresses that every time the inspiratory phase ends (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), and if there is no inspiratory pause required (i.e., inspiratoryPauseButton is NOT set), AND the RM (Recruitment Maneuver) is set by the GUI (RMButton), then a Recruitment Maneuver (RM) must start immediately after (i.e., RM must become true in the next state). This choice captures the conditional and causality described in the requirement.

This LTL property states: "Globally, whenever the inspiration phase ends (inspiratoryPhaseEnd) and inspiratory pause is NOT required (!inspiratoryPauseButton) and the Recruitment Maneuver is set by GUI (RMButton), then a Recruitment Maneuver (RM) should become active in the next step." This precisely captures the natural language requirement that, at the end of inspiration, given the preconditions, the RM maneuver should start, aligning each term with the available atomic propositions and their descriptions.

The requirement states that at the end of an inspiration phase, if an inspiratory pause is not required (i.e., inspiratoryPauseButton is NOT set), and the RM (Recruitment Maneuver) is set by the GUI (RMButton), then a Recruitment Maneuver (RM) must start. This can be captured by saying: Globally, whenever the end of the inspiratory phase occurs (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), and inspiratoryPauseButton is not set, and RMButton is set, then in the next step, RM must be active. This matches the pattern 'If trigger1 and not condition and trigger2, then in next step effect', which suggests template 41: G((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> (X bool_exp3)). Here, bool_exp1 = inspiratoryPhaseEnd, bool_exp2 = (!inspiratoryPauseButton & RMButton), and bool_exp3 = RM. The 'next step' (X) is chosen since the maneuver should start immediately after the trigger conditions are detected, as per typical real-time system assumptions.

This property formalizes the requirement that, at the end of an inspiration phase, if an inspiratory pause is not required AND if the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) is set by the GUI, the RM shall start. Here, 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd' indicates the end of the inspiration phase, '!inspiratoryPauseButton' means the inspiratory pause is not required by the GUI, 'RMButton' indicates the Recruitment Maneuver is set by the GUI, and 'RM' indicates the Recruitment Maneuver is active. Using template 41, we capture that whenever the end of inspiratory phase occurs and both conditions (no inspiratory pause required and RM set by GUI) are true, then RM is started immediately in the next step.

The property states that, at every end of an inspiratory phase ("inspiratoryPhaseEnd"), if the inspiratory pause is not required ("!inspiratoryPauseButton") and the Recruitment Maneuver is requested by the GUI ("RMButton"), a Recruitment Maneuver (RM) must be started. This fits the template: globally, when the trigger (the end of inspiration with those conditions) happens, the consequence (RM) must occur immediately. This captures the requirement from the natural language description that upon ending inspiration, if there is no pause request and the RM is set via GUI, RM must start.

This LTL property specifies that at every occurrence of the end of the inspiratory phase (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), if the inspiratory pause is not required (i.e., inspiratoryPauseButton is not pressed) and the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) is set by the GUI (RMButton is pressed), then a Recruitment Maneuver (RM) shall start (become active). This captures the requirement that at the conclusion of the inspiration phase, when a pause is NOT requested, and the recruitment maneuver is enabled via the user interface, the RM is triggered as required by CONT.43.

The natural language dictates that at the end of an inspiration phase (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), if the inspiratory pause is NOT required (which means inspiratoryPauseButton is NOT set), and the Recruitment Maneuver is set by the GUI (RMButton), then a Recruitment Maneuver (RM) shall start (i.e., become active). This is best represented with an LTL template that expresses: whenever (inspiratoryPhaseEnd AND NOT inspiratoryPauseButton AND RMButton) holds, then RM should hold. This implies a property of the form G ((A & B & C) -> D). Therefore, we use template 40. For the N/A-irrelevant bool_expressions, we set them as empty. All relevant context and atomic propositions have been captured.

The requirement is: At the end of an inspiration phase, IF inspiratory pause is NOT required and the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) is set by the GUI, THEN a Recruitment Maneuver shall start. Mapping this to atomic propositions: 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd' means the end of an inspiration phase, '!inspiratoryPauseButton' means inspiratory pause NOT required, 'RMButton' means Recruitment Maneuver is set by the GUI, and 'RM' means Recruitment Maneuver is active. The condition is that, whenever an inspiratory phase ends, and there is no inspiratory pause set, and Recruitment Maneuver is requested by GUI, then Recruitment Maneuver shall start (become active). The most appropriate template is an implication, always: IF (A and B and C) THEN D. This matches template 40: G ((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> bool_exp3).

So: bool_exp1 = inspiratoryPhaseEnd, bool_exp2 = ((!inspiratoryPauseButton) & RMButton), bool_exp3 = RM. This captures that upon end inspiratoryPhaseEnd, if RMButton is set and inspiratoryPauseButton is not set, RM must start (be active).

The given requirement is: At the end of the inspiratory phase (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), if no inspiratory pause is required (i.e., inspiratoryPauseButton is not set), and RM has been set by the GUI (RMButton), a Recruitment Maneuver (RM) should start. This is a conditional action where, when all three conditions are met, RM must become true (active) at that moment. The LTL property captures this as a global requirement: Whenever inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPauseButton & RMButton hold, RM must also be true at the same point. This matches template 40, expressing an implication for a conjunction of conditions.

The requirement states that at the end of the inspiratory phase, if the inspiratory pause is not active and the RM (Recruitment Maneuver) is set by the GUI, the RM should start. Mapping the concepts: 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd' indicates the end of inspiration; '!inspiratoryPause' captures that the pause is not required; 'RMButton' is the GUI set for RM; 'RM' denotes RM is active. The property is best captured by saying: Globally, if at the end of inspiration, there is no inspiratory pause and the RM is enabled by the GUI, then RM starts in the *next* step. Thus, the template 'G((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> (X bool_exp3))' applies. Here, bool_exp1 = inspiratoryPhaseEnd, bool_exp2 = (!inspiratoryPause) & RMButton, bool_exp3 = RM. N_DURATION is not used in this template.
